papers.title: Circulating Cell-Free DNA and Tumor Tissue Profiling in Metastatic Melanoma: A Comparative Study
papers.notes: Treatment-naive metastatic melanoma cohort. Paired EDTA plasma and tumor tissue collected at baseline before systemic therapy. Multi-institutional collaboration between Semmelweis (Budapest) and Lund.

papers.title: Establishment of a Melanoma Biospecimen Repository at NYU Langone Medical Center
papers.institution: NYU Langone Medical Center / Interdisciplinary Melanoma Cooperative Group (IMCG)
papers.notes: 974 blood specimens (EDTA) and 1142 FFPE blocks from melanoma patients. Large established biobank with formal access route via NYU IMCG. Treatment-naive status not uniformly documented for all specimens.

papers.title: The Lund Melanoma Biobank: A Resource for Translational Research
papers.access_route: Lund University biobank / Cancer Moonshot collaboration request
papers.notes: Lund Cancer Moonshot melanoma biobank. Tissue and blood stored at -80C. Formal biobank infrastructure with multi-institutional access potential. Exact N not specified in abstract.

papers.title: epiMelanoma: Epigenomic Characterisation of Melanoma Using K2-EDTA Plasma cfDNA
papers.access_route: consortium data access request / corresponding author
papers.notes: 199 participants with K2-EDTA plasma for cfDNA epigenomics. Consortium study — access route requires contact with lead PI. Storage conditions not specified in available metadata.

papers.title: Stage III Melanoma FFPE Biobank at the Netherlands Cancer Institute
papers.notes: Stage III melanoma treatment-naive FFPE specimens at NKI Amsterdam. Well-documented biobank with formal MTA-based access. Blood/plasma not mentioned — tissue only.

notes: Strong evidence base for melanoma tissue + K2-EDTA plasma. Three institutions have formal biobank infrastructure with documented access routes (NYU IMCG, Lund, NKI). The epiMelanoma consortium (PMC12370905) is the most direct K2-EDTA plasma match with 199 participants. Treatment-naive status is confirmed for Semmelweis/Lund (PMC11899103) and NKI (PMC11057279). NYU IMCG is the largest repository at 974+ specimens with mixed treatment history.
